title: Release Highlights 18.0.0
author: Rostislav Vítek
core_thesis: Shopsys 18.0.0 modernizes the admin (replacing a legacy custom theme with Tabler UI and cutting ~12,000 lines), ships merchant-facing promotional and compliance tooling (gifts, X+Y free, EU cooling-off withdrawal, QR bank transfers), and removes checkout/API friction via async email preparation and a 70-80% payload cut on product detail GraphQL.
target_audience: head of digital / ecommerce directors evaluating platform ROI
EU-regulated retailers needing consumer protection compliance

stats.claim: Lines of legacy admin code removed via Tabler UI migration
Payload reduction for store availability data in product detail GraphQL queries
Typical configurable withdrawal deadline after delivery
Example volume-based promotion pattern (pay for 3, get the 4th free)
Short-query search now returns basic name-based matches where previously it showed none

quotes.text: Reduced codebase by approximately 12 000 lines of legacy code
quotes.context: Bullet point summarizing the impact of the Tabler UI admin overhaul

quotes.text: This can reduce payload size by 70-80% for store availability data, resulting in faster API responses and improved (not only) mobile performance
quotes.context: Describing the GraphQL product detail optimization

quotes.text: The preparation step has now been moved out of the critical path, resulting in faster order creation and a lower risk of checkout failures.
Czech characters (č, ř, š, ž) now sort correctly when a Czech administrator is logged in

features.name: Administration Overhaul (Tabler UI)
features.benefit_line: A modern, responsive admin that replaces the custom legacy theme and is cheaper to maintain.
features.proof: Full redesign on Tabler UI framework, ~12,000 lines of legacy code removed, reworked modals, form templates, utility JS, and status colors (#3813)

features.name: Product Gift
features.benefit_line: Run gift-with-purchase campaigns that slot cleanly into cart, checkout, and emails.
features.proof: Configurable gift plans with validity periods, per-domain gift pricing, automatic 'Gift with Product' flagging, real-time cart updates, appearance in order summaries and confirmation emails (#4193)

features.name: Promotion X + Y for Free
features.benefit_line: Volume promos like '3 + 1 free' set directly on products to lift average order value.
features.proof: Per-product volume promotions, automatic flagging, multi-domain support, asynchronous flag updates (#4194)

features.name: Order Withdrawal from Contract
features.benefit_line: Built-in EU cooling-off compliance so customers can withdraw eligible orders from their account.
features.proof: Configurable withdrawal deadline (typically 14 days), per-domain instructions, new 'Withdrawn' order status, email notifications to customer and admin (#4246)

features.name: QR Payment for Bank Transfers
features.benefit_line: A bank transfer method where customers pay by scanning a QR code from their banking app.
features.proof: Embedded QR codes with IBAN, amount, and variable symbol in confirmation emails and order pages; per-domain account/IBAN/BIC configuration and dynamic placeholders (#4195)

features.name: Prevent Exceeding Available Stock
features.benefit_line: Kill overselling on products where stock accuracy actually matters.
features.proof: New 'Allow negative stock' per-product option; when disabled, cart quantities auto-adjust to available stock and the customer is notified (#4173)

features.name: Autocomplete Favorites
features.benefit_line: Curate what shows up before a customer types, and actually return something for 1-2 character queries.
features.proof: Admin-configurable favorite products, brands, and categories on autocomplete focus; simplified name-based search for short queries (#4215)

features.name: Domain Configuration with Path Fragment
features.benefit_line: Run all locales under one domain (example.com/cz, example.com/sk) instead of juggling subdomains.
features.proof: Path-based multi-domain URL structure, fewer hostnames to manage, simpler SEO signal for unified site structure (#4113)

features.name: Symfony Clock Integration
features.benefit_line: Deterministic time across the codebase — no more flaky time-dependent tests.
features.proof: Direct DateTime and DateTimeImmutable usage replaced with Symfony Clock across the codebase, full control over 'now' in tests (#4297)

features.name: DataSource Factories with Collation Support
features.benefit_line: Grids sort correctly in the administrator's language — including Czech diacritics.
features.proof: Factory pattern for all Grid DataSources; QueryBuilderDataSources auto-apply locale-specific collation so č, ř, š, ž sort correctly for Czech admins (#4135)

features.name: Asynchronous Order Email Preparation
features.benefit_line: Checkout no longer waits on template rendering and variable resolution.
features.proof: Email preparation moved out of the checkout critical path (previously only sending was deferred), reducing risk of checkout failures (#4266)

features.name: Reduced Product Detail Data Transfer
features.benefit_line: Faster product detail API responses, especially on mobile.
features.proof: GraphQL product detail returns only necessary store availability fields instead of full store info — 70-80% smaller payload for that data (#4324)

features.name: Storefront Design & Accessibility Pass
features.benefit_line: Sharper storefront UX, clearer layouts, and better a11y in the places customers actually touch.
features.proof: Order list thumbnails (#4213), new WYSIWYG text styles in CKEditor (#4208), redesigned contact info (#4221) and reset password (#4254) pages, refined autocomplete and navigation UX (#4215, #4230), banner slider and cart loading accessibility fixes (#4158, #4155), smoother scrolling on order/complaint pages (#4189), unified product image gallery (#4273)

stories.hook: We deleted ~12,000 lines of admin code and shipped a better admin.
stories.tension: The legacy custom-built admin theme had accumulated years of bespoke code that was expensive to maintain and felt dated on mobile.
stories.resolution: 18.0.0 replaces it with the Tabler UI framework — responsive across devices, standardized form templates, reworked modals and utility JS — with a net ~12,000-line reduction.

stories.hook: Checkout got faster because we moved email out of the critical path — for real this time.
stories.tension: Previously, only email sending was async; the template loading and variable resolution still ran synchronously during order placement, silently slowing checkout and adding failure risk.
stories.resolution: Order email preparation is now fully asynchronous (#4266), so order creation is faster and more resilient.

stories.hook: A single GraphQL field change cut product detail payloads by 70-80%.
stories.tension: Product detail queries were returning full store information when all the frontend actually needed was availability per store — heavy on mobile, pointless on the wire.
stories.resolution: Store availability in the product detail query now returns only what's needed (#4324), trimming 70-80% off that payload.

stories.hook: EU cooling-off rights are now a first-class feature, not a custom patch.
stories.tension: Consumer-protection withdrawal (the 14-day cooling-off period) is legally required across the EU but has typically been bolted on per-project.
stories.resolution: 18.0.0 ships Order Withdrawal from Contract (#4246) with a configurable deadline, per-domain instructions, a dedicated 'Withdrawn' order status, and notifications on both sides.

stories.hook: Flaky time-dependent tests have a single, boring root cause — and we fixed it.
stories.tension: Direct DateTime / DateTimeImmutable usage scattered across a large Symfony codebase makes 'now' impossible to control in tests.
stories.resolution: Symfony Clock is now the codebase-wide standard (#4297), enabling deterministic testing and killing a whole category of flaky tests.

takeaways: Dev: 18.0.0 is a cleanup release in the best sense — Symfony Clock everywhere, factory-based Grid DataSources with locale-aware collation, async order email preparation, and a leaner admin built on Tabler UI instead of a custom theme.

Merchant: You get the promotional and compliance features customers expect out of the box — gift-with-purchase, X+Y free volume promos, QR-coded bank transfers, strict stock control, and EU-compliant order withdrawal — without custom development.

Business: A modern responsive admin, 70-80% lighter product detail payloads, and faster checkout mean less platform drag on conversion and lower ongoing maintenance cost on a mid-to-enterprise footprint.
